data.title: Digital Scoreboards helped unveil Buford High School's new stadium with advanced LED displays.
data.description: On August 1, 2025, in Columbia, Digital Scoreboards, a prominent company in digital display solutions, revealed its contribution to the opening of Phillip Beard Stadium and Dexter Wood Field at Buford High School. The stadium showcases advanced technology and is set to enhance the sports experience for both the players and spectators.

data.title: Experity appoints Bobby Ghoshal as President & COO. Ghoshal to lead day-to-day operations for the national healthcare solutions leader.
data.description: On August 1, 2025, Experity, a prominent provider of on-demand healthcare services, revealed the selection of Bobby Ghoshal as the new President and Chief Operating Officer. Mr. Ghoshal will be responsible for overseeing daily operations and contributing to the company's strategic direction.

data.title: Grant awarded to Medicinal Media by Unlikely Collaborators to create 5 short stories focusing on mental health & perception.
data.description: On August 1, 2025, in Los Angeles, Unlikely Collaborators issued a grant to Medicinal Media for the creation of five innovative short-form content packages focusing on perception, mental health, and personal transformation. These packages will delve into various facets of these topics, aiming to provide unique insights and perspectives.

data.title: Subaru of America's July sales up 4.5%. Crosstrek has best July, Solterra's best month ever. Forester sales up 11.3%, Outback up 5.5%.
data.description: In July 2025, Subaru of America, Inc. (SOA) achieved record-breaking sales figures with 54,035 vehicles sold, marking a 4.5 percent increase from the previous year. The Crosstrek experienced its best July performance to date, while the Solterra had its best month ever. Additionally, Forester sales saw an 11.3 percent rise, and Outback sales increased by 5.5 percent. These impressive results were announced from Camden, N.J., on August 1, 2025, showcasing Subaru's strong performance in the market.

data.title: Financial adviser Ric Edelman increases bitcoin allocation from 1% to 40%, citing changing investment landscape.
data.description: Renowned financial expert Ric Edelman highlights the changing perception of Bitcoin, no longer seen solely as a speculative investment. With its growing acceptance, portfolio strategies are adapting.

data.title: Stocks plummet, gold rises amid tariff turmoil; U.S. indices plunge, setting for negative close; key tariffs include 25% on imports from India, 50% on Brazilian exports; July payrolls disappoint at 73,000 (below 110,000 expected). S&P 500 drops 1.2% to 6,260, Nasdaq 100 falls 1.3% to 22,900, Dow Jones sheds nearly 500 points; VIX up 18%. Fed rate cut expectations surge, now 80% chance of 25-basis-point cut in September. (297)
data.description: Major U.S. stock indices had a rough start to the month with a negative weekly close as the SPY ETF broke support levels amid renewed trade war concerns. President Donald Trump announced new tariffs on countries without trade agreements with the U.S., including 25% on imports from India, 39% from Switzerland, 20% from Taiwan, 30% from South Africa, 35% from Canada, and 50% on Brazilian exports. The July jobs report showed only a 73,000 job increase, well below the expected 110,000, with revisions slashing May and June payrolls by a combined 258,000. This indicated deeper issues in the labor market. The S&P 500 dropped 1.2% to 6,260, the Nasdaq 100 fell 1.3% to 22,900, and the Dow Jones lost nearly 500 points, or 1.1%. The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) spiked 18%, its largest one-day jump since April 10. There is now an 80% chance of a 25-basis-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve in September.
Professor Kimberly Clausing warns that U.S. tariff policy is damaging America’s credibility with key allies—raising doubts about whether the U.S. can be trusted in future trade negotiations.

data.title: FDA approved Novo Nordisk's Alhemo injection for hemophilia patients 12+ years without inhibitors, expanding treatment options.
data.description: The FDA has recently approved the Novo Nordisk Alhemo injection for patients 12 years and older with hemophilia A or B without inhibitors as a daily prophylaxis to reduce bleeding episodes. In December 2024, this approval was extended to include patients with inhibitors also. Many current hemophilia treatments for patients without inhibitors involve intravenous infusions, but Alhemo now provides a subcutaneous injection option. The medication works by blocking the TFPI protein, enhancing thrombin production to assist in blood clotting and prevent bleeding. This new approval offers a convenient and effective treatment alternative for patients with hemophilia.

data.title: Chemed Corp authorizes $300M for stock buyback, declares 60-cent quarterly dividend. Board of Directors has approved.
data.description: Chemed Corporation (NYSE: CHE) based in Cincinnati, revealed that their Board of Directors officially approved an extra $300 million for buying back stocks as part of the current share repurchase plan. This buyback initiative will be financed through a mix of operational cash flow and drawing from their revolving credit facility. The Board also declared a quarterly cash dividend of 60-cents per share on the company's capital stock, ensuring continued returns for shareholders.
Company purchased 100,000 of its own shares. Transaction completed today.

data.title: Wounded Warrior Project & NFL promote mental health awareness. 6 in 10 post-9/11 veterans struggle, highlights partnership efforts.
data.description: Morgan Fox, a defensive end playing for the Atlanta Falcons, and his father, an Army veteran, are challenging the stereotype that tough guys don't seek help. Based on data, mental health continues to be a significant issue for post-9/11 veterans, with over 60% of warriors facing challenges in this area. Their story underscores the importance of addressing mental health concerns within the veteran community.

data.title: Horizon Technology Finance to release Q2 2025 financial results. NASDAQ: HRZN provides secured loans to tech companies.
data.description: In Farmington, Connecticut, Horizon Technology Finance Corporation (NASDAQ: HRZN), a prominent specialty finance company associated with Monroe Capital, disclosed its intention to report financial results for the second quarter. Horizon provides secured loans to various companies in industries such as technology, life science, healthcare, information services, and sustainability. Stay tuned for updates from Horizon.

data.title: New nonprofit initiative launched in Boulder, Colo. aiming to empower leaders for global systemic change.
data.description: A unique community educational platform and nonprofit project, aimed at empowering leaders to promote systemic change on a global scale, was launched in Boulder, Colorado on August 1, 2025. The initiative addresses various pressing global issues such as climate change, systemic inequality, etc.
